preview_text:
The model rates Switzerland as favorite with a 41% win probability, but the margin over Algeria is modest—a 13-point gap. Algeria's 28% chance reflects genuine competitive viability in this neutral-venue encounter. The draw sits at 31%, suggesting the model sees meaningful uncertainty in the outcome.

The win-draw-loss spread indicates a relatively tight contest. Switzerland's advantage is clear but not commanding; a draw remains more likely than an Algeria victory, yet the three-outcome distribution shows no outcome with overwhelming backing. The match should prove competitive rather than one-sided.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both lean more bullish on Switzerland than the model, crediting the favorite with 47% rather than 41%—a gap of roughly 6 percentage points. Both markets trim Algeria's chances to 24%, narrower than the model's 28%. The two external sources align closely with each other. The dashboard makes no claim as to which reading is more accurate.
